commit.message:
Merge origin/main into main

Summary:
- merge the latest origin/main updates into local main
- keep workspace repo summaries on the shared workspaceinfra git
  snapshot helpers introduced upstream
- preserve stronger unborn HEAD coverage by checking staged and
  untracked files while keeping missing-workspace skip behavior

Rationale:
- local workspace summary fixes need to sit on top of the current main
  branch before further work continues
- resolving onto the shared helper layer avoids duplicating unborn HEAD
  handling across chat and orchestrator paths

Tests:
- PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/infra/workspace ./internal/chat ./internal/orchestrator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: openase
repository.description: Ticket-Driven Automated Software Engineering. OpenASE is an all-in-one platform that turns tickets into working code — AI agents automatically pick up tickets, execute workflows on your machines, and deliver results with full traceability.

commit.message:
fix: update terraform viewer file ref synchronously

Co-authored-by: codex@openai.com

fix(workspace): handle unborn git heads in summaries

Summary:
- fall back to symbolic-ref when workspace repos have an unborn HEAD
- diff against the empty tree so staged files are summarized before the
  first commit exists
- add chat and orchestrator tests covering staged and untracked files in
  freshly initialized repos

Rationale:
- workspace and run completion summaries should still report useful repo
  state while a repo is initialized but not yet committed
- using the empty tree preserves accurate added and removed counts without
  scattering special-case checks through the summary path

Tests:
- PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/chat ./internal/orchestrator

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Revamp simple-mode rule management

feat: add hierarchical merge for issue-split to cap sub-issue count

Closes #276

After directory-based grouping, progressively merge communities up one
directory level until the count falls within the target range (--max-splits,
default 8). Root-level overflow merges the smallest communities into an
"other" group. Merged groups get human-readable titles (e.g. "CLI Commands").

commit.message:
Improve remote pair classification and tagged result discovery

Update the remote path in bintoken.py to normalize yes/no responses through parse_yesno_response, send diagnostic token logprob output to stderr, and score each CSV pair in both word orders before printing a consolidated YES/NO result.

Extend compare_results.py filename parsing so discovery accepts optional result tags after the host segment and includes that tag in discovery keys, which prevents tagged result files from colliding with untagged runs.

Co-authored-by: Codex GPT-5.4 <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: words
repository.description: using an LLM to evaluate word association

commit.message:
Refactor flipped-pair retry handling into shared evaluation logic

Move the pair-flipping and async retry-on-NO-or-None policy into a
shared helper in common.py so eval_prompt.py and evalpair.py use the
same behavior.

For eval_prompt.py, make flipped retry the default evaluation policy,
record both original and flipped raw/normalized responses, and roll both
attempts into per-pair timing so reporting reflects the extra work.
Also surface flipped wins in compact output.

For evalpair.py, replace the bespoke async flip/retry path with the
shared helper while preserving the existing result formatting and retry
tracking. Reuse the shared pair flip helper in the sync path as well.

Co-authored-by: Codex GPT-5.4 <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Harden phase-two regression tests

Tighten the two-pass runWith() regression fixtures so they only treat
parsed === undefined as phase 1 and explicitly assert that phase 2
ran. This makes both tests fail if phase-two refinement is skipped,
which is the behavior the suite is supposed to guard.

Related to https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/243

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Make SourceContext phase explicit

Replace the old inferred SourceContext mode model with an explicit
phase policy. Runners now decide phase-two collection solely from
context.phase, cache phase-one snapshots for single-pass contexts,
and let two-pass contexts refine or clear non-empty phase-one
annotations predictably.

This rewrites the regression tests around runWith()/runWithSync(),
updates built-in env/config contexts, and migrates docs, examples,
and CHANGES.md to the single-pass/two-pass terminology for
Optique 1.0.0.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/243

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(cli): prefer newest Claude session timestamp

Use the newest available Claude session timestamp when deciding whether a
session is older than the configured threshold. This avoids deleting recent
sessions when `sessions-index.json` contains a stale `modified` value but a
newer `fileMtime` is available.

Also accept digit-only timestamp strings in the shared date parser so older
provider metadata keeps resolving to real dates instead of falling back to
Unix epoch behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Make phase two annotations authoritative

Two-phase context parsing used to merge phase-one annotations back into
the final parse. That let stale values survive even when the same
context returned an empty annotation object in phase 2, which also broke
fallback to later contexts.

Treat each context's phase-two annotations as the final snapshot for the
second parse, add regression coverage for async and sync runners, and
document the contract in the public API docs and changelog.

Fixes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/231

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add cursor pagination to project activity (#625)

* feat(activity): add cursor pagination to project activity

Summary:
- add stable `before` cursor parsing and paged activity responses
- update repo, service, HTTP, and OpenAPI layers for activity pagination
- add Activity page load-more handling with realtime-safe dedupe and tests

Rationale:
- offset-style paging would duplicate or skip activity while new events
  keep arriving, so the activity API now pages on `created_at` and `id`
- loading one page at a time keeps the Activity screen responsive while
  preserving existing search, filtering, and realtime refresh behavior
- regenerated API artifacts keep the backend contract and frontend types in
  sync for the new pagination metadata

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/domain/catalog ./internal/service/catalog ./internal/repo/catalog ./internal/httpapi ./internal/chat
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm --dir web exec vitest run src/lib/features/activity/components/activity-page.test.ts src/lib/features/board/components/board-page-controls.test.ts src/lib/features/board/components/board-page.test.ts src/lib/features/board/components/board-page-runtime-and-drawer.test.ts src/lib/features/dashboard/components/org-dashboard.test.ts
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm --dir web run check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(activity): split page helpers to satisfy web budgets

Summary:
- move Activity page state helpers into dedicated browser-side modules
- keep the page component and test files under the enforced web file budgets
- preserve the validated load-more and realtime merge behavior unchanged

Rationale:
- the web CI gate enforces hard file-size limits on new or changed files
- extracting the helper logic keeps the feature shippable without weakening
  the existing lint and budget checks

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(activity): smooth load more reveal

Summary:
- add a visible loading panel and minimum animation window for
  Activity page history pagination
- keep the Load more button disabled while older events are being
  fetched and reveal the pending state immediately
- extend the Activity page test to cover the loading state before
  older history is appended

Rationale:
- the rework feedback called out that Load more felt abrupt and gave
  weak feedback while older activity was loading
- showing a temporary loading treatment makes the interaction feel
  intentional without disturbing the existing pagination behavior

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm --dir web exec prettier --check src/lib/features/activity/components/activity-page.svelte src/lib/features/activity/components/activity-page.test.ts
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm --dir web exec vitest run src/lib/features/activity/components/activity-page.test.ts
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm --dir web run check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(activity): split loading visuals from page

Summary:
- move the initial Activity skeleton into a dedicated loading component
- move the Load more pending panel into its own placeholder component
- keep the Activity page under the repo max-lines budget after the
  rework animation update

Rationale:
- the CI-aligned file budget guard rejected the rework once the page
  component exceeded the hard line limit
- extracting the view-only loading blocks keeps the page easier to
  scan without changing behavior

Tests:

commit.message:
docs: avoid hidden module links

Summary:
- replace hidden module references in the driver docs and changelog with
  plain wording so ExDoc does not try to link internal modules
- keep the published documentation aligned with the application-owned
  driver boundary introduced in the previous refactor

Rationale:
- hidden-module links generate noisy doc warnings even though the wording
  itself is correct
- plain public-language descriptions are a better fit for internal-only
  helpers that are intentionally excluded from docs

Validation:
- mix docs

  notification rule dialogs into dedicated components.
- Restore organization member section props for invitations and

  file budgets so frontend validation can pass.

chore(changelog): record refactor hash

Summary:
- replace the unreleased changelog placeholders with the short SHA of the
  substantive driver refactor commit

Rationale:
- the repo requires release-note-worthy changelog entries to carry a real
  short hash before handoff

Validation:
- git rev-parse --short HEAD

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(driver): move sample drivers out of lib

Summary:
- move the concrete EK1100, EL1809, EL2809, and ATV320 drivers out
  of lib and into test support, leaving the published package with the
  driver contracts, runtime helpers, and Default driver only
- document the application-owned driver boundary in the core driver and
  capture docs, and remove the old integration-support delegate wrappers
- replace reflective simulator slave callback probing with explicit
  overridable behaviour defaults, and add focused ATV320 driver and
  simulator integration coverage

Rationale:
- concrete device models are fixture code and should not ship as part of
  the library runtime surface
- explicit simulator behaviour defaults are a cleaner contract than
  probing module load state before every optional callback dispatch

Validation:
- mix test --no-start test/ethercat/slave/driver_test.exs \
  test/ethercat/driver/atv320_test.exs \
  test/ethercat/slave_description_test.exs \
  test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs \
  test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/06_mailbox_abort_sdo_test.exs \
  test/integration/simulator/52_atv320_driver_cia402_safeop_recovery_test.exs

commit.message:
ASE-117: add secret rollout safeguards and hygiene (#623)

* chore(secrets): add rollout masking and audit safeguards

Summary:
- add secret activity events for create, rotate, bind, unbind,
  disable, and delete across project and organization APIs
- mask secret-like machine env vars and runtime secret resolution,
  preserve masked machine values on PATCH, and remove secret values
  from run completion summaries
- expose security-settings migration and rollout diagnostics, update
  frontend surfaces, and regenerate OpenAPI artifacts

Rationale:
- keep the scoped secret rollout backwards compatible while legacy
  provider and machine config migrates to first-class secret bindings
- close remaining audit and masking gaps so APIs, UI responses, and
  runtime summaries do not leak raw secret material

Tests:
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/domain/catalog ./internal/domain/activityevent ./internal/httpapi ./internal/orchestrator
- PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" corepack pnpm --dir web exec vitest run src/lib/features/activity/event-catalog.test.ts src/lib/features/settings/components/security-settings.test.ts

  (regenerated expected API contract artifacts; diff remained because
  the updated artifacts were not yet committed)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:
- extend secret hygiene coverage to exercise the remaining masking and
  merge helper branches so domain coverage stays at 100%
- format the updated machine masking route test and replace the
  runtime secret resolve fixture with a synthetic non-credential value
- keep the secret resolve assertions focused on redaction semantics
  while avoiding gosec false positives in test fixtures

Rationale:
- the CI-aligned gate caught formatting, coverage, and lint regressions
  after syncing the branch with the latest base
- fixing those tests keeps the rollout safeguards change aligned with
  the repo's strict backend coverage and security-lint requirements

commit.message:
ref: Add optional vision model support (#169)

Make the vision model explicit and decouple image analysis from the main
agent model.

This adds optional `AI_VISION_MODEL` config, wires Slack attachment
handling through the vision service, and treats vision as an opt-in
path. When `AI_VISION_MODEL` is set, uploaded images are summarized
through that model and passed to the main agent as prompt text, while
cached thread image summaries are reused when available. If required
image analysis fails, we stop on the existing internal-error path
instead of sending the agent incomplete image context.

I considered keeping image handling on `AI_MODEL` as an implicit
fallback, but that keeps coding and vision responsibilities coupled and
makes runtime behavior harder to reason about. The explicit split lets
us run a coding-focused primary model without silently re-enabling image
understanding.

This also updates init scaffolding, environment/docs references, and
Slack/config tests for the new opt-in behavior, and pins local Node to
24 so local setup matches the supported development environment.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

limit client-socket helper to evloop builds

Keep h2o_quic_create_client_socket in the non-libuv path, matching its callers and avoiding checkdepends build failures in libh2o/libuv configurations.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Remove unused figures and update acknowledgements

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(browser-demo): clip overlay to video bounds

Summary:
- map overlay vectors into the contained video rectangle instead of the
  full canvas and clip drawing to the visible video area
- switch the browser demo to sample every decoded frame by default and
  compute vectors synchronously to avoid stale frame races
- add regression coverage for contained-video geometry and projected
  vector coordinates

Rationale:
- the overlay canvas was sized to the full viewer card, so vectors could
  extend into letterboxed space when the video used object-fit: contain
- the previous worker pipeline and default stride of 3 made updates look
  sparse and could leave older vectors on newer frames
- low-resolution synchronous estimation is a better fit for this MVP's
  per-frame refresh requirement than an async hop that can miss the
  displayed frame

Tests:
- npm test
- node browser validation probe (desktop/mobile screenshots, rvfc cadence
  check, synthetic clip check)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(order): restore variant snapshots and payment finalization (#1134)

* fix(order): restore variant snapshots and payment finalization

* fix(admin): show read-only order item details

* fix(payments): harden verify route and server-side assurance fee

- Do not short-circuit completed transactions when the order is unreconciled;
  fall through so webhook-crash scenarios still finalize the order.
- Add amount/currency verification to the verify route, mirroring the
  webhook's checks, to prevent partial-pay exploits.
- Guard order update behind the transaction claim so concurrent requests
  cannot double-update the order when they lose the race.
- Recompute assurance_fee server-side using a fixed rate constant instead
  of trusting the client-supplied monetary value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(payments): guard shipping_status regression in verify route

Only advance shipping_status to 'processing' when the order is still
in 'pending' state — prevents regressing orders already moved to
'shipped' or 'delivered'.

Also exclude public/ from Biome lint (static marketing HTML assets,
not application code — noSvgWithoutTitle / useHtmlLang are irrelevant).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(pdp): dependent variant filtering — prevent impossible RAM/storage combos (#1138)

* fix(import): delegate to worker instead of inline processing in after()

Replace kickoffImportJob with startImportJob in all 4 import route
after() callbacks (finalize, multipart upload, commit, notify-customers).

startImportJob uses a clean strategy:
- Production with worker secret: delegates to /api/import-jobs/worker
  (runs in its own request context with maxDuration=300)
- Production without secret: throws to catch misconfiguration
- Non-production (localhost): falls back to inline processing

Previously, after() always ran processImportJobById inline first, which
claims the job then does heavy I/O. On Vercel, the function timeout
kills the process mid-work, orphaning the job with no recovery path.

Also adds logger.warn in triggerImportWorker when IMPORT_JOB_WORKER_SECRET
is missing as a defensive layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(import): harden worker kickoff recovery

* fix(import): only delegate to worker in production + fix test name

* fix(import): only delegate to worker in production environments

* fix(import): delegate to worker whenever secret is configured, not only in production

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(pdp): add dependent variant filtering to prevent impossible combos

Selecting RAM now filters available storage to only valid pairs that
exist in product_variants. Impossible combinations are shown as
disabled with strikethrough styling. Selecting a new axis value
auto-clears any sibling selection that is no longer reachable.

Also consolidates S22–S25 Ultra to ram+storage variant pairs (data
migration run directly in Supabase — no migration file needed as
this was a data backfill, not a schema change).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(pdp): add dependent variant filtering to prevent impossible combos

Selecting RAM now filters available storage to only valid pairs that
exist in product_variants. Impossible combinations are shown as
disabled with strikethrough styling. Selecting a new axis value
auto-clears any sibling selection that is no longer reachable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): type onSelectAttribute mock explicitly to satisfy strict TS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(operator): add workflow orchestration lane

Summary:
- add a workspace-level workflow orchestration endpoint and cockpit lane for multi-session supervision
- keep orchestration controls available from orchestration payload fallbacks and pin the contract in backend/frontend tests
- update shipped-truth docs for the long-running orchestration surface

Rationale:
- Seraph needed denser multi-session workflow control instead of forcing operators through one inspected run at a time
- the orchestration lane needed explicit regression coverage so lead-run controls do not disappear when /api/workflows/runs is sparse

Tests:
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_operator_api.py -q
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npx vitest run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- cd frontend && npm test -- --run
- cd frontend && npm run build
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(operator): keep team control plane workspace scoped

Summary:
- stop session-scoping the cockpit team control plane fetch
- preserve request scope while synthesizing control-plane handoff entries
- pin the corrected backend and cockpit contracts in tests

Rationale:
- a review pass found that unscoped control-plane requests could silently drop follow-ups after the approval loop shadowed the request session id
- the cockpit was also presenting a thread-scoped payload as a workspace-level team control plane, which contradicted the shipped contract

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/operator.py backend/tests/test_operator_api.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_operator_api.py -q
- cd frontend && NODE_OPTIONS=--experimental-require-module npx vitest run src/components/cockpit/CockpitView.test.tsx
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
test(ase-118): sync security settings route expectations

Summary:
- update the security settings HTTP tests so project credential endpoints assert the new
  project-only scope semantics
- stop sending or expecting org scope on project credential save and delete calls

Rationale:
- org credential management now lives on dedicated org routes, so the project route tests were
  asserting an outdated cross-scope contract
- aligning the tests with the routed behavior keeps backend coverage focused on the actual API
  surface we ship

Tests:
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/httpapi -run 'TestSecuritySettingsRoute(SavesManualCredential|DeletesCredential)$' -count=1
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/httpapi

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
test(ase-118): update github auth stub repository

Summary:
- teach the github auth service test stub the new `GetOrganizationContext` repository method
- derive org test context from the existing project fixture unless a test supplies one explicitly

Rationale:
- the merged org credential feature widened the repository contract used by the github auth
  service, which left the unit-test stub behind
- keeping the stub in sync lets the backend suite validate the real service behavior without
  weakening the interface or skipping coverage

Tests:
- PATH="$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH" go test ./internal/service/githubauth

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ase-118): restore access migration regression copy

Summary:
- format the updated ticket fixture and org credential files so frontend CI stays clean
- restore the disabled-mode access card copy expected by the IAM migration E2E flow

Rationale:
- the merged branch passed type checks after the contract fix, but the web gate still failed on
  formatting drift and a regression in the access migration walkthrough wording
- keeping the copy aligned with the regression spec preserves the intended navigation guidance from
  project settings into `/admin` and org admin

Tests:
- PLAYWRIGHT_WEB_SERVER_MODE=dev PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" corepack pnpm --dir web exec playwright test tests/e2e/iam-settings-migration.spec.ts --reporter=dot --quiet

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ase-118): align frontend contract usage

Summary:
- update frontend credential flows to use the current project and org
  GitHub credential API signatures
- add the new `pull_request_urls` ticket field to typed frontend fixtures
  so contract-driven tests match the merged API schema

Rationale:
- the merge introduced newer ticket and credential contracts than several
  frontend callers and fixtures expected
- fixing the contract mismatches restores the repo CI gate without backing
  out the landed product changes

Tests:
- PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" corepack pnpm --dir
  web exec svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json --output machine
  --threshold error

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
